meta.description: Plans confounder control, variable adjustment logic, and bias mitigation strategies at the protocol stage for clinical, epidemiologic, translational, observational, and biomarker studies. Always use this skill when a user needs to identify major confounders, decide which variable

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced.

methodological_ground.detail: Collider bias, mediator misclassification, and immortal time bias all explicitly handled

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 2, design planning only

functional_suitability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

reliability.note: Strong handling of uncertain variable roles; gap: no fallback when user provides no variable list at all

agent_usability.note: Full marks (16/16); no significant issues detected.

security.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

maintainability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

agent_specific.note: Strong critical posture enforced; description well-targeted but could be shortened and add natural trigger phrases

inputs.label: Baseline CRP as predictor of sepsis mortality — what to adjust for

assertions.text: Variable role map produced with explicit confounder/mediator/collider classifications
Time order established before any adjustment recommendation
Post-baseline variables excluded from baseline adjustment set with warning
Minimum sufficient control set defined with reasoning, not just variable list
Residual confounding acknowledged as non-removable in Section J

inputs.label: Retrospective EHR cohort — identify bias and confounders before protocol finalization

assertions.text: Immortal time bias risk assessed for EHR time-zero structure
Selection bias and confounding by indication both identified
Control strategy recommendation justified for the specific design context
Critical weak points section present with specific protocol revision recommendation
No fabricated variable availability or dataset fields assumed

inputs.label: Case-control study of smoking and lupus — which variables to match on

assertions.text: Variables on causal pathway explicitly excluded from matching recommendation
Overmatching risk and analytic consequences stated for each matching candidate
Alternative to matching (restriction or multivariable adjustment) presented as comparison
Collider bias risk checked for proposed matching variables
No recommendation to 'adjust for everything available'

inputs.label: Mixed variable list including post-treatment response — role classification challenge

assertions.text: Post-treatment variables correctly classified as post-baseline and excluded from baseline adjustment
Role-uncertain variables labeled rather than forced into a false classification
Mediator vs confounder boundary explicitly addressed for ambiguous variables
Prediction variables correctly distinguished from confounders
Caution against statistical complexity masking bias explicitly stated

inputs.label: Pressure-test a complex observational protocol with propensity score plan already drafted

assertions.text: Propensity score plan reviewed with explicit justification check (design, data quality, covariate set)
Critical review of proposed propensity model identifies whether covariate set is appropriate
Residual bias after propensity score method acknowledged
Bias mitigation actions provided per major risk identified
Practical next step section provides most useful immediate action

key_strengths: Variable role classification before any adjustment recommendation is a methodologically sound and rare discipline in AI-assisted protocol review
Explicit collider bias detection and prohibition of mediator adjustment are strong differentiators from generic statistics advice
Hard Rules effectively prevent the most common critical errors: adjust-for-everything, post-baseline variables in baseline set, propensity score by default
Eight reference modules with precise step-level mapping provide comprehensive bias-sensing coverage

recommendations.title: No fallback when user provides no variable list at all
recommendations.problem: When a user asks for confounder and bias planning without supplying any variable list or study design details, the skill lacks a structured minimum-clarification protocol to extract what is needed before proceeding.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section shows examples with variable lists, but does not define the minimum required inputs before the full analysis can begin.
recommendations.fix: Add a clarification gate: if no variable list or study design is provided, ask for: (1) exposure variable, (2) outcome variable, (3) suspected major confounders, and (4) data source type before proceeding to full bias analysis.

recommendations.title: Description is long and may over-trigger on general statistics or prediction modeling questions
recommendations.problem: The description includes phrases like 'prediction modeling' and 'observational studies', which may cause this skill to trigger on questions that are better served by a dedicated statistical modeling or prediction skill.
recommendations.root_cause: Description optimized for coverage over precision.
recommendations.fix: Add a negative-trigger qualifier: 'not for pure predictive feature selection or machine learning model building without bias-control purpose' in the description to reduce false-positive triggering.
